title: arachne australis
side_effects: Arachne australis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, which may manifest as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Overuse of the plant can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, improper handling of the arachne australis plant can cause skin irritation and burns, particularly if the sap comes into contact with open wounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also exacerbate underlying health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease.
